desc: Palermo is Sicily's layered capital, where Arab-Norman churches, Spanish-era palaces, and vibrant street markets sit within walking distance of the Tyrrhenian coast. The city is big, gritty, and deeply rewarding for travelers interested in architecture, street food, and day trips to beaches, Monreale, or Cefalù.
hls: Arab-Norman monuments (Palatine Chapel, cathedral, Norman Palace)
Legendary street-food culture in Ballarò and Vucciria
Grand Belle Époque avenue life around Teatro Massimo and Politeama
Easy access to Mondello beach and Monte Pellegrino viewpoints
Unique fusion of Byzantine, Islamic, and Latin art traditions
Strong base for side trips to Monreale, Cefalù, and Segesta

todo.name: Palazzo dei Normanni & Cappella Palatina
todo.desc: Visit Palermo's most important royal complex for glittering mosaics and Arab-Norman design.

todo.name: Palermo Cathedral
todo.desc: Tour crypts, royal tombs, and rooftop terraces with views over the old city.

todo.name: Teatro Massimo
todo.desc: Join a guided tour or evening performance at Italy's largest opera house.

todo.name: Ballarò Market
todo.desc: Snack on panelle, arancine, and grilled street food in Palermo's liveliest daytime market.

todo.name: Quattro Canti & Piazza Pretoria
todo.desc: Explore the Baroque crossroads and sculptural fountains in the historic center.

todo.name: Catacombe dei Cappuccini
todo.desc: See Palermo's famous historic catacombs with preserved mummies and burial galleries.

todo.name: Monreale Cathedral day trip
todo.desc: Take a short bus or taxi ride to admire one of Sicily's finest Byzantine mosaic interiors.

todo.name: Mondello Beach
todo.desc: Spend a half day swimming and relaxing at Palermo's classic city beach.

wifi.note: Good in central accommodations and modern cafés; speeds vary in older palazzi.

prac.fltNote: Direct Gatwick links are often seasonal; outside summer, many itineraries connect through Rome, Milan, or other European gateways.
prac.visa: Italy is in the Schengen Area; many non-EU visitors (e.g., US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.
alerts: Traffic can be chaotic in central Palermo; walking and official taxis are often easier than self-driving in the core.
Popular market lanes get crowded at lunch; keep valuables secure in dense areas.

sweet: April to June and late September to early November give warm weather, active markets, and fewer crowds than midsummer on both city streets and Mondello beach.
